conditionalExam.diagnostic_target: insulin delivery failure, infusion-site infection, or device-related precipitant

initialTests.action: Interpret with local lab ranges. Reference anchors: One-step 75-g OGTT for GDM: fasting >=92, 1-hour >=180, or 2-hour >=153 mg/dL. Two-step approach if used: 50-g screen often >=130-140 mg/dL; 100-g Carpenter-Coustan thresholds fasting 95, 1-hour 180, 2-hour 155, 3-hour 140 mg/dL, usually 2 abnormal values. A1c: normal <5.7%; prediabetes 5.7-6.4%; diabetes >=6.5%. Fasting plasma glucose: normal <100 mg/dL; prediabetes 100-125; diabetes >=126. 2-hour 75-g OGTT: normal <140 mg/dL; prediabetes 140-199; diabetes >=200. Random plasma glucose >=200 mg/dL with classic symptoms supports diabetes diagnosis. Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects.

treatmentOptions.action: Escalate to endocrinology or the relevant specialty when results are severe, discordant, recurrent, treatment-limiting, or require localization, surgery, genetic testing, or high-risk medication.
treatmentOptions.rationale: Links confirmed Gestational Diabetes results, severity, contraindications, and patient context to guideline-backed treatment options and monitoring.
treatmentOptions.diagnostic_target: Escalate to endocrinology or the relevant specialty when results are severe, discordant, recurrent, treatment-limiting, or require localization, surgery, genetic testing, or high-risk medication.: result or bedside context that changes treatment intensity, monitoring, referral, or disposition for Gestational Diabetes.
treatmentOptions.management_change: Escalate to endocrinology or the relevant specialty when results are severe, discordant, recurrent, treatment-limiting, or require localization, surgery, genetic testing, or high-risk medication.
treatmentOptions.likelihood_ratio_note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
treatmentOptions.limitations: This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Gestational Diabetes.

reference_values: One-step 75-g OGTT for GDM: fasting >=92, 1-hour >=180, or 2-hour >=153 mg/dL.
Two-step approach if used: 50-g screen often >=130-140 mg/dL; 100-g Carpenter-Coustan thresholds fasting 95, 1-hour 180, 2-hour 155, 3-hour 140 mg/dL, usually 2 abnormal values.
Fasting plasma glucose: normal <100 mg/dL; prediabetes 100-125; diabetes >=126.
Random plasma glucose >=200 mg/dL with classic symptoms supports diabetes diagnosis.
Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects.
decision_steps: Confirm pregnancy status and gestational age; screen high-risk patients early for overt diabetes and otherwise test at 24-28 weeks using the local one-step or two-step strategy.
Interpret one-step 75-g OGTT using fasting >=92, 1-hour >=180, or 2-hour >=153 mg/dL; one abnormal value diagnoses GDM in that strategy.
If using two-step testing, route abnormal 50-g screen to diagnostic 100-g OGTT and apply local Carpenter-Coustan or NDDG thresholds.
After delivery, schedule 75-g OGTT at 4-12 weeks and long-term diabetes surveillance.
Confirm that Gestational Diabetes is the selected clinical intent, then screen for red flags before routine outpatient testing.
Order the first-line tests in sequence: Early risk testing if high risk; 24-28 week one-step 75-g OGTT or local two-step strategy; Postpartum 75-g OGTT at 4-12 weeks.

Interpret numeric results against the workup cutoffs and local assay ranges: One-step 75-g OGTT for GDM: fasting >=92, 1-hour >=180, or 2-hour >=153 mg/dL. Two-step approach if used: 50-g screen often >=130-140 mg/dL; 100-g Carpenter-Coustan thresholds fasting 95, 1-hour 180, 2-hour 155, 3-hour 140 mg/dL, usually 2 abnormal values.

If the initial result is equivocal, discordant with the phenotype, or affected by medication, acute illness, pregnancy, age, sex, or assay interference, repeat or confirm using the next guideline test before committing to long-term therapy.
treatment_options: Start nutrition therapy, activity as obstetrically appropriate, glucose monitoring, and obstetric fetal-growth coordination.
Add insulin when lifestyle therapy does not meet pregnancy glucose targets or if fasting/postprandial values are persistently above local goals.
Use metformin or glyburide only within local obstetric/endocrine policy and shared decision-making about placental transfer and failure rates.
Escalate vomiting, ketones, dehydration, severe hyperglycemia, hypertension, or fetal/maternal danger signs urgently.
Treat unstable presentations first using the urgent escalation rule for Gestational Diabetes; draw time-sensitive labs only if doing so does not delay stabilization.
For confirmed Gestational Diabetes, choose therapy based on severity, comorbidities, pregnancy/fertility context, contraindications, patient preference, and local specialty pathway.
Monitor treatment response with the condition-specific clinical endpoint plus the most relevant labs or imaging listed in this workup.
Escalate to endocrinology or the relevant specialty when results are severe, discordant, recurrent, treatment-limiting, or require localization, surgery, genetic testing, or high-risk medication.

Gestational Diabetes: diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup

Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: One-step 75-g OGTT for GDM: fasting >=92, 1-hour >=180, or 2-hour >=153 mg/dL. Two-step approach if used: 50-g screen often >=130-140 mg/dL; 100-g Carpenter-Coustan thresholds fasting 95, 1-hour 180, 2-hour 155, 3-hour 140 mg/dL, usually 2 abnormal values.

Important mimics/exclusions: stress or steroid hyperglycemia, type 1/LADA versus type 2 diabetes, DKA/HHS versus uncomplicated hyperglycemia, hypoglycemia/drug effect, renal disease, infection, pregnancy, and secondary endocrine causes.
OGTT threshold crossing changes maternal/fetal monitoring and therapy.
Postpartum abnormal OGTT changes long-term prevention.
Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care.
Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment.

root.description: Activate when structured or extractable context matches Gestational Diabetes triggers or the clinician selects workup gestational_diabetes_v1.

endpoint_missing_context.description: Route here when the patient context needed to activate Gestational Diabetes is absent or cannot be extracted.

gestational_diabetes_concurrent_data_bun.description: Obtain Gestational Diabetes diagnostic/severity/treatment-safety data together rather than as a one-step chain.

Two-step approach if used: 50-g screen often >=130-140 mg/dL; 100-g Carpenter-Coustan thresholds fasting 95, 1-hour 180, 2-hour 155, 3-hour 140 mg/dL, usually 2 abnormal values. A1c: normal <5.7%; prediabetes 5.7-6.4%; diabetes >=6.5%. Fasting plasma glucose: normal <100 mg/dL; prediabetes 100-125; diabetes >=126. 2-hour 75-g OGTT: normal <140 mg/dL; prediabetes 140-199; diabetes >=200. Random plasma glucose >=200 mg/dL with classic symptoms supports diabetes diagnosis. Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects. children.edgeLabel: Missing exact Gestational Diabetes pathway context: symptoms, exam, vitals, labs/results, medications, comorbidities, demographics, pregnancy/applicability, and workup findings

criteria.description: Route here when the patient context needed to activate Gestational Diabetes is absent or cannot be extracted.

children.action: Before choosing a non-emergency Gestational Diabetes branch, document: presenting trigger, onset/trajectory, focused exam, vital signs, threshold labs/results, medication/allergy context, comorbidities, demographics, pregnancy/applicability status, and current workup findings.

children.label: Gestational Diabetes: obtain concurrent diagnostic, severity, and treatment-safety data
children.edgeLabel: Selected Gestational Diabetes workup or documented findings: How many weeks pregnant are you, and has pregnancy dating, fetal growth, or obstetric context changed diabetes screening or treatment safety?; Any history of prior gestational diabetes or macrosomia that changes Gestational Diabetes risk, test interpretation, or treatment safety?; Any pre-pregnancy diabetes, prior A1c/glucose abnormality, PCOS, obesity, steroid exposure, or strong family history that changes gestational diabetes classification or treatment intensity?; What is the current home glucose pattern if available, and how has it changed from baseline?; Ketones, vomiting/dehydration, severe hyperglycemia.; Hypertension or preeclampsia features.; Early risk testing if high risk.

criteria.description: Obtain Gestational Diabetes diagnostic/severity/treatment-safety data together rather than as a one-step chain.

children.action: Obtain together: Early risk testing if high risk.; 24-28 week one-step 75-g OGTT or local two-step strategy.; Postpartum 75-g OGTT at 4-12 weeks.; Compare with prior results and repeat discordant nonurgent endocrine tests using correct timing, local assay, and interference precautions.; Gestational diabetes safety tests: route the local OGTT strategy with gestational age, review home glucose pattern if available, and check ketones, electrolytes/anion gap, creatinine, and acid-base status for vomiting, dehydration, severe hyperglycemia, or insulin-deficiency symptoms.; Gestational diabetes screening/diagnostic OGTT using the local one-step or two-step strategy and gestational-age context.; Glucose monitoring plan; ketones, electrolytes, renal function, and acid-base assessment when vomiting, dehydration, severe hyperglycemia, or insulin deficiency is possible.; One-step 75-g OGTT for GDM: fasting >=92, 1-hour >=180, or 2-hour >=153 mg/dL.; Measure blood pressure; Measure heart rate; Measure current weight; Measure waist circumference; Document mental status; fasting >=92; hour >=180; or 2-hour >=153 mg/dL; g screen often >=130; 140 mg/dL; fasting >=92; hour >=180; or 2-hour >=153 mg/dL.

criteria.description: Classify Gestational Diabetes against cited thresholds and condition-specific rules: Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: One-step 75-g OGTT for GDM: fasting >=92, 1-hour >=180, or 2-hour >=153 mg/dL. Two-step approach if used: 50-g screen often >=130-140 mg/dL; 100-g Carpenter-Coustan thresholds fasting 95, 1-hour 180, 2-hour 155, 3-hour 140 mg/dL, usually 2 abnormal values.: fasting >=92, hour >=180, or 2-hour >=153 mg/dL, g screen often >=130, 140 mg/dL | Early risk testing if high risk.: fasting >=92, hour >=180, or 2-hour >=153 mg/dL, g screen often >=130, 140 mg/dL, normal <5.7% | 24-28 week one-step 75-g OGTT or local two-step strategy.: 24-28 week, fasting >=92, hour >=180, or 2-hour >=153 mg/dL, g screen often >=130, 140 mg/dL | Postpartum 75-g OGTT at 4-12 weeks.: 4-12 weeks, fasting >=92, hour >=180, or 2-hour >=153 mg/dL, g screen often >=130, 140 mg/dL | Compare with prior results and repeat discordant nonurgent endocrine tests using correct timing, local assay, and interference precautions.: fasting >=92, hour >=180, or 2-hour >=153 mg/dL, g screen often >=130, 140 mg/dL, normal <5.7%

guideline_cutoffs.criteria_text: Postpartum 75-g OGTT at 4-12 weeks. Interpret with local lab ranges. Reference anchors: One-step 75-g OGTT for GDM: fasting >=92, 1-hour >=180, or 2-hour >=153 mg/dL. Two-step approach if used: 50-g screen often >=130-140 mg/dL; 100-g Carpenter-Coustan thresholds fasting 95, 1-hour 180, 2-hour 155, 3-hour 140 mg/dL, usually 2 abnormal values. A1c: normal <5.7%; prediabetes 5.7-6.4%; diabetes >=6.5%. Fasting plasma glucose: normal <100 mg/dL; prediabetes 100-125; diabetes >=126. 2-hour 75-g OGTT: normal <140 mg/dL; prediabetes 140-199; diabetes >=200. Random plasma glucose >=200 mg/dL with classic symptoms supports diabetes diagnosis. Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects. children.label: Gestational Diabetes: initiate treatment/disposition for the active threshold-defined branch
children.edgeLabel: Gestational Diabetes treatment/disposition indicated by: fasting >=92; hour >=180; or 2-hour >=153 mg/dL; g screen often >=130; 140 mg/dL; normal <5.7%; Start nutrition therapy, activity as obstetrically appropriate, glucose monitoring, and obstetric fetal-growth coordination.; Add insulin when lifestyle therapy does not meet pregnancy glucose targets or if fasting/postprandial values are persistently above local goals.; Use metformin or glyburide only within local obstetric/endocrine policy and shared decision-making about placental transfer and failure rates.; Escalate vomiting, ketones, dehydration, severe hyperglycemia, hypertension, or fetal/maternal danger signs urgently.; OGTT threshold crossing changes maternal/fetal monitoring and therapy.; Postpartum abnormal OGTT changes long-term prevention.; Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care.

criteria.description: Use the Gestational Diabetes treatment branch only when cited diagnostic/severity thresholds are satisfied and required treatment-safety data are available.

children.label: Gestational Diabetes: de-escalate, stop, or narrow when objective criteria normalize
children.edgeLabel: Gestational Diabetes objective response supports narrowing, stopping, tapering, or continuing: fasting >=92; hour >=180; or 2-hour >=153 mg/dL; g screen often >=130; OGTT threshold crossing changes maternal/fetal monitoring and therapy.; Postpartum abnormal OGTT changes long-term prevention.; Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care.; Start nutrition therapy, activity as obstetrically appropriate, glucose monitoring, and obstetric fetal-growth coordination.

parallel_actions: Start nutrition therapy, activity as obstetrically appropriate, glucose monitoring, and obstetric fetal-growth coordination.
Add insulin when lifestyle therapy does not meet pregnancy glucose targets or if fasting/postprandial values are persistently above local goals.
Use metformin or glyburide only within local obstetric/endocrine policy and shared decision-making about placental transfer and failure rates.
Escalate vomiting, ketones, dehydration, severe hyperglycemia, hypertension, or fetal/maternal danger signs urgently.
Treat unstable presentations first using the urgent escalation rule for Gestational Diabetes; draw time-sensitive labs only if doing so does not delay stabilization.
OGTT threshold crossing changes maternal/fetal monitoring and therapy.
Postpartum abnormal OGTT changes long-term prevention.
Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care.
